description: You are a science educator at a natural history museum preparing an interactive demonstration of Galileo Galilei's discovery of Jupiter's moons. Recreate Galileo's observations from Florence, Italy (43.7696°N, 11.2558°E, altitude 50m) for two nights: January 7, 1610, at 19:00 UTC and January 8, 1610, at 19:00 UTC. The view must have the atmosphere and ground landscape disabled, constellation lines enabled, and be centered on Jupiter with the Galilean moons (Io, Europa, Ganymede, and Callisto) visible. Capture a screenshot for each night and record the observation dates and a description of the moon position changes in the educator's demonstration notes at /home/ga/Desktop/galileo_demo_notes.txt.

metadata.data_source: Galileo Galilei (1610) Sidereus Nuncius; Florence Italy coordinates
